title: [FIRRTL] (unsupported Verilog construct) 2D wires in YoSyS
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input clock: Clock
    input arst: AsyncReset
    output fsm_170_next: UInt<4>
    reg fsm_170_state: UInt, clock with: (reset => (arst, UInt(0)))
    when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(0)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(1)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(1)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(2)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(2)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(3)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(3)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(4)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(4)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(5)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(5)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(6)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(6)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(7)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(7)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(8)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(8)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(9)
    else when eq(fsm_170_state, UInt<4>(9)) :
      fsm_170_next <= UInt<4>(0)
    else :
      fsm_170_next <= fsm_170_state
    fsm_170_state <= fsm_170_next
```

Compiled with `firtool` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
...
  always @(posedge clock or posedge arst) begin	// a_top_mod.fir:7:5
    if (arst)	// a_top_mod.fir:7:5
      fsm_170_state <= 4'h0;	// a_top_mod.fir:7:5, :8:28
    else	// a_top_mod.fir:7:5
      fsm_170_state <= _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:29:20, :30:19
  end // always @(posedge or posedge)
...
  wire [15:0][3:0] _T_0 = {{fsm_170_state}, {fsm_170_state}, {fsm_170_state}, {fsm_170_state}, {fsm_170_state}, {fsm_170_state}, {4'h0}, {4'h9}, {4'h8}, {4'h7}, {4'h6}, {4'h5}, {4'h4}, {4'h3}, {4'h2}, {4'h1}};	// a_top_mod.fir:8:{10,28}, :9:23, :10:15, :11:23, :12:15, :13:23, :14:15, :15:23, :16:15, :17:23, :18:15, :19:23, :20:15, :21:23, :22:15, :23:23, :24:15, :25:23, :26:15, :29:20
  assign _T = _T_0[fsm_170_state];	// a_top_mod.fir:8:10, :29:20
  assign fsm_170_next = _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:3:10, :29:20
endmodule

```

`Yosys 0.9+3911 (git sha1 4b31223e, clang 11.0.1 -fPIC -Os)` reports this error (wire [15:0][3:0] _T_0 ... line):

```
a_top_mod.fir.mfc.v:60: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected '[', expecting TOK_ID or '#'
```
